root.label: China announces export controls on rare earth processing equ
root.description: China announces export controls on rare earth processing equipment to allied nations

n1.label: Rare earth oxide prices +25%
n1.description: Restricting exports of processing equipment chokes the marginal supply of separated/refined rare earth oxides outside China, since allied nations cannot rapidly stand up midstream capacity. Spot prices for NdPr, Dy, Tb spike on supply scarcity.

evidence.note: VanEck Rare Earth/Strategic Metals ETF as price proxy
broad commodity PPI for context

n2.label: MP Materials equity +20%
n2.description: Western rare earth miners and processors with existing ex-China capacity (MP Materials, Lynas, Energy Fuels) become scarce strategic assets. Equity re-rates on expected pricing power and likely government support contracts.

evidence.note: MP Materials, US rare earth miner
Lynas, Australian processor

n3.label: EV/wind OEM input cost +3-5%
n3.description: Permanent magnet motors in EVs and direct-drive wind turbines depend on NdFeB magnets sourced largely from Chinese midstream. With allied processors unable to scale, OEMs face higher BOM costs and potential allocation shortfalls. Margin compression at Tesla, GM, Vestas, Siemens Energy.

evidence.note: EV magnet exposure
Vestas wind turbine OEM
GM EV gross margin sensitivity

n4.label: US DPA/DoD funding for domestic rare earth processing
n4.description: Direct policy reaction: the US invokes Defense Production Act Title III and DoD Industrial Base funding to subsidize domestic separation and magnet plants. EXIM and DOE loan programs accelerate disbursements to MP, Lynas USA, Energy Fuels, and magnet maker projects.

evidence.note: USA Rare Earth, beneficiary of subsidy flow

evidence.ref: DoD rare earth supply chain awards
evidence.note: precedent of Title III awards to MP and Lynas

edges.mechanism: event triggers Rare earth oxide prices +25%
event triggers MP Materials equity +20%
event triggers EV/wind OEM input cost +3-5%
event triggers US DPA/DoD funding for domestic rare earth processing

mp_materials_equity_surge_e03ee9.description: MP Materials share price rallies sharply as the Pentagon equity stake signals government demand backstop and reduces financing risk for rare earth processing expansion.

evidence.note: MP shares rallied roughly 71 percent post event with a 9.2 sigma peak deviation, an extreme single-name re-rating consistent with a direct government equity backstop.
Rare earth ETF moved 12.6 sigma above pre-event mean, confirming the move is sector-validating and not idiosyncratic noise.
Lynas, a peer rare earth processor, rallied 2.7 sigma, corroborating a sector re-rating tied to the Pentagon signal.

rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.label: Rare earth miner equities rally
rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.description: Peer rare earth and critical minerals producers such as Energy Fuels and Lynas see sympathy gains as the deal signals broader US government willingness to subsidize domestic supply chains.

evidence.note: MP Materials itself rose about 71 percent, anchoring the parent move and confirming the catalyst magnitude.
Energy Fuels surged about 44 percent with peak z above 14, showing the policy signal extended to adjacent critical minerals names.
Rare earth miner ETF rallied roughly 21 percent post event with peak deviation over 12 sigma, direct confirmation of sector-wide sympathy bid.

critical_minerals_etf_inflows_0566dd.label: Critical minerals ETF inflows
critical_minerals_etf_inflows_0566dd.description: Passive and thematic ETFs tracking critical minerals and rare earths receive net inflows as retail and institutional allocators chase the sector re-rating.

evidence.note: Critical minerals ETF surged over 12 sigma above pre-event mean within 12 days, consistent with strong inflows alongside price appreciation.
Adjacent critical minerals ETF rallied 5.7 sigma, confirming the thematic ETF channel extends beyond pure rare earth names.
Anchor holding rallied 9.2 sigma, mechanically lifting REMX NAV and drawing momentum-driven allocators.

edges.mechanism: Sympathy gains in peer rare earth miners lift critical minerals ETF NAVs and attract net inflows as the sector re-rates on US subsidy signaling.

supporting_data.note: Critical minerals ETF surged over 12 sigma above pre-event mean within 12 days, consistent with strong inflows alongside price appreciation.

Lynas equity dropped 2.7 sigma, which is counterintuitive for the contract awardee and likely reflects broader risk-off in rare earth names rather than oxide price discovery.
Rare earth miners ETF fell 2.2 sigma, directionally consistent with softer expected oxide prices, but equity moves are an indirect proxy.

wti_crude_oil_price_declines_ab6629.label: WTI crude oil price declines
wti_crude_oil_price_declines_ab6629.description: Oil prices fall as global growth pessimism triggered by the manufacturing supply shock reduces expected energy demand.

evidence.note: 10y yield dropped over 6 sigma, a clear growth-fear signal during the window
WTI fell over 7 sigma with peak roughly 2 days after the yield trough, consistent with growth-driven demand repricing
Oil ETF confirms WTI move with similar magnitude and timing
Energy equities sold off 3.7 sigma, corroborating demand-side interpretation rather than idiosyncratic oil supply shock

vix_spikes_on_supply_chain_shock_fear_60.label: VIX spikes on supply chain shock fear
vix_spikes_on_supply_chain_shock_fear_60.description: Equity implied volatility rises as investors reprice uncertainty around rare earth-dependent US technology and defense supply chains.

evidence.note: VIX rose about 1.3 sigma over the window, a modest but directionally consistent spike in implied volatility
tool_error: empty series
Rare earth miners ETF rose, confirming the rare earth supply chain channel was active
SPY sold off sharply, corroborating risk-off conditions that typically accompany VIX spikes

broad_usd_index_strengthens_066c5f.label: Broad USD index strengthens
broad_usd_index_strengthens_066c5f.description: The trade-weighted USD index rises as capital flows into dollar safe-haven assets amid escalating US-China trade conflict signals.

evidence.note: Broad USD index rose roughly 3 sigma within 11 days of the visit, a clear safe-haven appreciation.
EUR weakened about 2 sigma vs USD, consistent with broad dollar bid.
CNY depreciated nearly 2 sigma vs USD, consistent with trade war stress and dollar strength.
Dollar bullish ETF rose nearly 3 sigma, corroborating broad USD strength.

copper_futures_decline_80b565.label: Copper futures decline
copper_futures_decline_80b565.description: Copper prices fall as CNY weakness raises the effective cost of imports for Chinese buyers while signaling weaker domestic demand and manufacturing activity.

evidence.note: Broad USD strengthened by roughly 3 sigma over the window, a proxy for CNY weakness pressure.
Copper ETF fell about 2.7 sigma post-event, consistent with the proposed channel and timed within 16 days.
China large-cap equities dropped about 2 sigma, corroborating the weaker domestic demand signal.

high_yield_energy_credit_spreads_widen_b.label: High-yield energy credit spreads widen
high_yield_energy_credit_spreads_widen_b.description: Leveraged US shale producers face tighter margins at lower oil prices, pushing HY energy OAS wider as default risk reprices.

evidence.note: WTI fell over 7 sigma from pre-event mean, a severe shock to shale economics.
tool_error: empty series
Broad energy equity decline of 3.7 sigma corroborates sector stress that typically transmits to HY energy OAS.
Shale-heavy E&P equity ETF dropped 3.8 sigma, consistent with sharp repricing of leveraged producer credit risk.

cny_weakens_past_6_90_per_usd_bc8128.label: CNY weakens past 6.90 per USD
cny_weakens_past_6_90_per_usd_bc8128.description: The offshore and onshore yuan depreciate as USD strength and trade-war escalation fears pressure Chinese authorities to allow or accept a weaker fix.

evidence.note: Broad USD index rose 3 sigma above pre-event mean within 11 days, confirming dollar strength channel.
USD/CNY rose nearly 2 sigma, crossing 6.90 within 9 days, directly consistent with the proposed link.
Yuan ETF declined 1.8 sigma, corroborating CNY weakness via a market-traded proxy.
China large-cap equities fell over 2 sigma, consistent with capital outflow pressure that amplifies CNY depreciation.

em_equity_index_underperforms_caf959.label: EM equity index underperforms
em_equity_index_underperforms_caf959.description: MSCI EM equity benchmarks decline as a stronger USD tightens financial conditions in emerging markets and raises dollar-denominated debt service costs.

evidence.note: Broad USD index rose 3 sigma above pre-event mean within 11 days, confirming dollar strength channel.
tool_error: get_series failed: Bad Request.  The series does not exist.
EM equity ETF fell 2.1 sigma with peak drawdown reached in 3 days, consistent with USD-driven outflows.
China large-cap ETF fell 2.07 sigma alongside EEM, reinforcing the EM equity weakness signal.

2y_ust_yield_drops_sharply_29e88a.label: 2y UST yield drops sharply
2y_ust_yield_drops_sharply_29e88a.description: 2-year Treasury yields fall as markets price in Fed rate cuts in response to growth fears from a potential rare earth supply shock and escalating trade war.

evidence.note: 2y yield fell 46bp in 14 days, a 7.3 sigma move, directly confirming the candidate.
Fed target unchanged, so the 2y move reflects pure repricing of future cuts rather than realized policy.
Curve steepened as the front end rallied harder than the long end, consistent with rate-cut pricing.
Long Treasuries rallied sharply, corroborating a broad duration bid driven by growth and policy fears.

10y_ust_yield_falls_on_growth_fear_7c135.label: 10y UST yield falls on growth fear
10y_ust_yield_falls_on_growth_fear_7c135.description: 10-year Treasury yields decline as rare earth export restriction fears mark down long-run US growth and manufacturing output expectations.

evidence.note: 10y yield fell about 42 bp from a 2.49 percent pre-event mean, a 6.4 sigma move consistent with strong flight to quality and growth markdown
2y yield fell 46 bp, indicating broad duration rally and rate cut repricing, not just term premium
Curve steepened modestly at the peak as front end fell faster, consistent with cut expectations dominating growth fear
Long bond ETF rallied over 6 percent, corroborating the duration bid

energy_sector_equities_underperform_b5e3.label: Energy sector equities underperform
energy_sector_equities_underperform_b5e3.description: US energy producers and oilfield services stocks decline as lower WTI prices compress expected cash flows and capex guidance.

evidence.note: WTI fell over 7 sigma versus pre-event, a sharp parent move.
Broad energy ETF dropped 3.7 sigma, confirming sector-level transmission.
E&P focused ETF fell nearly 4 sigma, consistent with revenue compression.
Oilfield services ETF fell 3 sigma, capex channel intact.

aud_usd_declines_on_china_demand_fears_0.label: AUD/USD declines on China demand fears
aud_usd_declines_on_china_demand_fears_0.description: The Australian dollar weakens as CNY depreciation signals slowing Chinese demand, reducing expectations for Australian commodity exports.

evidence.note: AUD/USD fell about 2 sigma from 0.700 to 0.692 within one day of the event, consistent with co-movement.
CNY weakened roughly 1.9 sigma past 6.90 per USD, confirming the parent move.
Broad USD strength of 3 sigma is a confounder, meaning some AUD weakness reflects USD beta rather than pure CNY transmission.
Aussie dollar ETF dropped 1.8 sigma, corroborating the spot AUD weakness.
Chinese large-cap equities sold off 2 sigma, reinforcing the China-demand-fear channel that drives AUD.

cad_weakens_vs_usd_on_oil_linkage_028db5.label: CAD weakens vs USD on oil linkage
cad_weakens_vs_usd_on_oil_linkage_028db5.description: The Canadian dollar, tightly linked to crude export revenues, depreciates against the USD as WTI falls and risk sentiment sours.

evidence.note: WTI fell over seven sigma from pre-event mean, a clear oil shock during the window.
USD/CAD rose 2.66 sigma, consistent with CAD weakening as oil dropped, peaking in 9 days.
CAD currency ETF fell 2.25 sigma, corroborating CAD depreciation versus USD.
Oil ETF confirms the magnitude of the crude decline tracked by WTI.

tips_breakeven_inflation_falls_d74fdf.label: TIPS breakeven inflation falls
tips_breakeven_inflation_falls_d74fdf.description: Inflation breakevens compress as lower growth expectations reduce the inflation outlook, amplifying the nominal yield decline.

evidence.note: 10y nominal yield dropped roughly 42 bps, providing the parent move that breakevens partially tracked.
10y real yield fell about 25 bps, meaning roughly 17 bps of the 42 bps nominal decline came from breakevens, consistent with a meaningful inflation expectations channel.
5y5y forward breakeven fell about 17 bps with a 3.2 sigma peak deviation, confirming inflation expectations compressed materially over the window.

root.label: China announces export controls on rare earth processing equ
root.description: China announces export controls on rare earth processing equipment to allied nations

n1.label: Rare earth oxide prices +25%
n1.description: Restricting exports of processing equipment chokes the marginal supply of separated/refined rare earth oxides outside China, since allied nations cannot rapidly stand up midstream capacity. Spot prices for NdPr, Dy, Tb spike on supply scarcity.

evidence.note: VanEck Rare Earth/Strategic Metals ETF as price proxy
broad commodity PPI for context

n2.label: MP Materials equity +20%
n2.description: Western rare earth miners and processors with existing ex-China capacity (MP Materials, Lynas, Energy Fuels) become scarce strategic assets. Equity re-rates on expected pricing power and likely government support contracts.

evidence.note: MP Materials, US rare earth miner
Lynas, Australian processor

n3.label: EV/wind OEM input cost +3-5%
n3.description: Permanent magnet motors in EVs and direct-drive wind turbines depend on NdFeB magnets sourced largely from Chinese midstream. With allied processors unable to scale, OEMs face higher BOM costs and potential allocation shortfalls. Margin compression at Tesla, GM, Vestas, Siemens Energy.

evidence.note: EV magnet exposure
Vestas wind turbine OEM
GM EV gross margin sensitivity

n4.label: US DPA/DoD funding for domestic rare earth processing
n4.description: Direct policy reaction: the US invokes Defense Production Act Title III and DoD Industrial Base funding to subsidize domestic separation and magnet plants. EXIM and DOE loan programs accelerate disbursements to MP, Lynas USA, Energy Fuels, and magnet maker projects.

evidence.note: USA Rare Earth, beneficiary of subsidy flow

evidence.ref: DoD rare earth supply chain awards
evidence.note: precedent of Title III awards to MP and Lynas

evidence.note: MP Materials peak deviation was negative, with the largest move a drawdown later in the window as broader equity risk and growth stock derating dominated NdPr tailwinds.
Lynas rallied roughly 22 percent above the pre window mean within 93 days, consistent with NdPr price strength flowing into Australian producer equity.
The rare earth miner ETF also showed its largest deviation as a drawdown, indicating the equity channel was overwhelmed by macro risk-off rather than NdPr fundamentals over this window.

evidence.note: Battery and EV materials basket dropped over 8 sigma, indicating broader EV materials demand expectations were repriced lower.
Rare earth and strategic metals equities sold off sharply over the window, consistent with a demand outlook reset alongside the price peak.
Lead EV OEM equity declined materially, consistent with margin and volume concerns that would motivate magnet content reduction.

Lynas, Australian processor

mp_materials_equity_surge_e03ee9.label: MP Materials equity surge
mp_materials_equity_surge_e03ee9.description: MP Materials share price rallies sharply as the Pentagon equity stake signals government demand backstop and reduces financing risk for rare earth processing expansion.

evidence.note: MP shares rallied roughly 71 percent post event with a 9.2 sigma peak deviation, an extreme single-name re-rating consistent with a direct government equity backstop.
Rare earth ETF moved 12.6 sigma above pre-event mean, confirming the move is sector-validating and not idiosyncratic noise.
Lynas, a peer rare earth processor, rallied 2.7 sigma, corroborating a sector re-rating tied to the Pentagon signal.

rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.label: Rare earth miner equities rally
rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.description: Peer rare earth and critical minerals producers such as Energy Fuels and Lynas see sympathy gains as the deal signals broader US government willingness to subsidize domestic supply chains.

evidence.note: MP Materials itself rose about 71 percent, anchoring the parent move and confirming the catalyst magnitude.
Energy Fuels surged about 44 percent with peak z above 14, showing the policy signal extended to adjacent critical minerals names.
Rare earth miner ETF rallied roughly 21 percent post event with peak deviation over 12 sigma, direct confirmation of sector-wide sympathy bid.

critical_minerals_etf_inflows_0566dd.label: Critical minerals ETF inflows
critical_minerals_etf_inflows_0566dd.description: Passive and thematic ETFs tracking critical minerals and rare earths receive net inflows as retail and institutional allocators chase the sector re-rating.

evidence.note: Critical minerals ETF surged over 12 sigma above pre-event mean within 12 days, consistent with strong inflows alongside price appreciation.
Adjacent critical minerals ETF rallied 5.7 sigma, confirming the thematic ETF channel extends beyond pure rare earth names.
Anchor holding rallied 9.2 sigma, mechanically lifting REMX NAV and drawing momentum-driven allocators.

rare_earth_and_mining_equity_rally_5da45.label: Rare earth and mining equity rally
rare_earth_and_mining_equity_rally_5da45.description: ETFs and equities tied to rare earth miners and critical materials producers outperform as the strategic supply chain narrative draws capital into the sector.

evidence.note: Rare earth miners ETF rallied roughly 66 percent from pre-event mean, with an extreme z-score confirming sector-wide revaluation.
Lithium and battery materials ETF rallied roughly 36 percent, showing the rally extended to adjacent critical minerals.
MP Materials itself rallied roughly 165 percent, anchoring the re-rating narrative.

rare_earth_oxide_spot_price_shift_da4c51.label: Rare earth oxide spot price shift
rare_earth_oxide_spot_price_shift_da4c51.description: Prices for separated heavy rare earth oxides such as dysprosium and terbium respond to the signal that US non-China separation capacity is being funded, altering supply expectations.

evidence.note: MP Materials fell roughly 1.9 sigma on the event day, consistent with traders pricing in additional non-China separation supply, but n_post is 1 so this is a same-day reaction only.
Lynas equity dropped 2.7 sigma, which is counterintuitive for the contract awardee and likely reflects broader risk-off in rare earth names rather than oxide price discovery.
Rare earth miners ETF fell 2.2 sigma, directionally consistent with softer expected oxide prices, but equity moves are an indirect proxy.

2y_ust_yield_drops_sharply_29e88a.label: 2y UST yield drops sharply
2y_ust_yield_drops_sharply_29e88a.description: 2-year Treasury yields fall as markets price in Fed rate cuts in response to growth fears from a potential rare earth supply shock and escalating trade war.

evidence.note: 2y yield fell 46bp in 14 days, a 7.3 sigma move, directly confirming the candidate.
Fed target unchanged, so the 2y move reflects pure repricing of future cuts rather than realized policy.
Curve steepened as the front end rallied harder than the long end, consistent with rate-cut pricing.
Long Treasuries rallied sharply, corroborating a broad duration bid driven by growth and policy fears.

rare_earth_miner_equity_rally_c691bc.label: Rare earth miner equity rally
rare_earth_miner_equity_rally_c691bc.description: Listed rare earth producers such as MP Materials and Lynas Rare Earths saw equity prices surge as NdPr revenue realizations and margin expectations reset sharply higher.

evidence.note: MP Materials peak deviation was negative, with the largest move a drawdown later in the window as broader equity risk and growth stock derating dominated NdPr tailwinds.
Lynas rallied roughly 22 percent above the pre window mean within 93 days, consistent with NdPr price strength flowing into Australian producer equity.
The rare earth miner ETF also showed its largest deviation as a drawdown, indicating the equity channel was overwhelmed by macro risk-off rather than NdPr fundamentals over this window.

mp_materials_equity_surge_e03ee9.label: MP Materials equity surge
mp_materials_equity_surge_e03ee9.description: MP Materials share price rallies sharply as the Pentagon equity stake signals government demand backstop and reduces financing risk for rare earth processing expansion.

evidence.note: MP shares rallied roughly 71 percent post event with a 9.2 sigma peak deviation, an extreme single-name re-rating consistent with a direct government equity backstop.
Rare earth ETF moved 12.6 sigma above pre-event mean, confirming the move is sector-validating and not idiosyncratic noise.
Lynas, a peer rare earth processor, rallied 2.7 sigma, corroborating a sector re-rating tied to the Pentagon signal.

rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.label: Rare earth miner equities rally
rare_earth_miner_equities_rally_3eb7d9.description: Peer rare earth and critical minerals producers such as Energy Fuels and Lynas see sympathy gains as the deal signals broader US government willingness to subsidize domestic supply chains.

evidence.note: MP Materials itself rose about 71 percent, anchoring the parent move and confirming the catalyst magnitude.
Energy Fuels surged about 44 percent with peak z above 14, showing the policy signal extended to adjacent critical minerals names.
Rare earth miner ETF rallied roughly 21 percent post event with peak deviation over 12 sigma, direct confirmation of sector-wide sympathy bid.

critical_minerals_etf_inflows_0566dd.label: Critical minerals ETF inflows

evidence.note: Critical minerals ETF surged over 12 sigma above pre-event mean within 12 days, consistent with strong inflows alongside price appreciation.
Adjacent critical minerals ETF rallied 5.7 sigma, confirming the thematic ETF channel extends beyond pure rare earth names.
Anchor holding rallied 9.2 sigma, mechanically lifting REMX NAV and drawing momentum-driven allocators.

ree_spot_prices_surge_08b9ff.label: REE spot prices surge

vix_spike_on_supply_shock_fear_968cb9.description: Equity implied volatility surges as markets price in potential disruption to rare earth supply chains critical for defense, EVs, and semiconductors.

evidence.note: VIX peaked roughly 9 points above pre event mean within 7 days, a textbook vol shock consistent with a supply scare.
HY OAS widened sharply within one day, corroborating a broad risk repricing rather than an isolated equity move.
Semis ultimately rallied into the window close, suggesting the vol spike was a transient fear pulse that faded as policy details emerged.
Rare earth miners rallied 5 days post event, confirming the REE supply channel is the proximate driver.

hy_credit_spreads_widen_847919.label: HY credit spreads widen
hy_credit_spreads_widen_847919.description: High yield OAS expands as leveraged issuers in materials, auto, and tech hardware sectors face margin risk from REE input cost shocks.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened roughly 41 bps within one day of the announcement, an 8 sigma move versus the prior month.
BBB IG spreads widened about 6 bps, confirming credit risk repricing extended into investment grade.
VIX spiked 9 points, consistent with a broader risk-off impulse coincident with the spread widening.
HY ETF dropped over 1 dollar at peak, corroborating the OAS widening through price action.
Second HY ETF confirms the move was broad based across HY index constructions.

10y_ust_yield_falls_on_safety_bid_7a9746.label: 10y UST yield falls on safety bid
10y_ust_yield_falls_on_safety_bid_7a9746.description: Flight-to-quality demand lifts Treasuries as investors de-risk from equities and credit into sovereign bonds amid the supply shock.

evidence.note: 10y yield fell 14bp with a 2.7 sigma move within 13 days, consistent with safety bid
Volatility spike confirms the risk-off backdrop driving the safety bid
Curve flattened modestly, consistent with long-end bid outpacing front end
Long duration Treasury ETF rallied over 5 sigma, corroborating duration demand
SPY actually rose post-event, weakening the pure risk-off narrative and capping sensitivity

usd_broad_index_strengthens_a3ef19.label: USD broad index strengthens
usd_broad_index_strengthens_a3ef19.description: The broad trade-weighted USD index rises as risk-off capital flows favor dollar-denominated safe assets over EM and commodity currencies.

evidence.note: Broad USD index rose roughly 3 sigma over the window, peaking 21 days post event, consistent with safe haven flows.
EUR/USD fell 3.2 sigma, corroborating dollar strength via the largest DXY component.
Sharp VIX spike supports the risk-off channel underlying USD strength.
Dollar bullish ETF rose 3.8 sigma, confirming the move in tradable form.
EM equities also rose, slightly weakening the pure risk-off narrative but not the USD move itself.

wti_crude_oil_drops_on_demand_fears_235b.label: WTI crude oil drops on demand fears
wti_crude_oil_drops_on_demand_fears_235b.description: Crude falls as the implied volatility surge signals an expected pullback in industrial and EV-related energy demand, compounded by risk-off liquidation of commodity longs.

evidence.note: VIX spiked nearly 14 sigma above pre-event baseline, peaking 7 days post-event, confirming a sharp risk-off impulse
WTI fell over 4 sigma with peak drawdown coinciding exactly with the VIX peak at day 7, consistent with risk-off liquidation
Crude ETF corroborates the WTI move with matching sign, magnitude, and 7-day time-to-peak
SPY ultimately rallied with peak at day 20, suggesting the risk-off was concentrated in commodities rather than broad equities, which moderately weakens the pure risk-off interpretation

leveraged_loan_secondary_prices_drop_720.label: Leveraged loan secondary prices drop
leveraged_loan_secondary_prices_drop_720.description: Floating-rate leveraged loans to materials and auto-parts issuers reprice lower as HY spread widening signals deteriorating credit quality and CLO managers trim exposure.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened sharply, confirming the parent move and providing the impulse for loan repricing.
BBB OAS widened over 3 sigma, showing the credit repricing extended into investment grade and supports cross asset credit deterioration.
Senior loan ETF fell nearly 4 sigma over 12 days, directly evidencing leveraged loan secondary price weakness consistent with the proposed channel.
HY bond ETF fell almost 6 sigma, corroborating broad credit risk repricing that typically drags loan prices alongside bonds.

hy_energy_and_materials_etf_outflows_4bd.label: HY energy and materials ETF outflows
hy_energy_and_materials_etf_outflows_4bd.description: Retail and institutional flows exit HY ETFs concentrated in materials and industrials as spread widening triggers stop-loss redemptions.

evidence.note: HY OAS spiked over 8 sigma above pre-event mean within 1 day, a clear shock to high yield credit.
BBB IG OAS widened 3 sigma, showing contagion into adjacent credit but smaller magnitude than HY.
HYG ETF dropped 5.7 sigma, consistent with NAV decline and outflow pressure on the largest HY ETF.
JNK fell 5.2 sigma in parallel with HYG, confirming the move is broad across HY ETF complex rather than idiosyncratic.

ig_to_hy_fallen_angel_risk_rises_0ce0f9.label: IG-to-HY fallen angel risk rises
ig_to_hy_fallen_angel_risk_rises_0ce0f9.description: BBB-rated auto and tech hardware issuers face ratings watch downgrades as margin compression from REE cost shocks raises leverage ratios toward HY territory.

gold_spot_price_surges_aa45cb.label: Gold spot price surges
gold_spot_price_surges_aa45cb.description: Gold rallies as real yields drop on the Treasury bid and investors seek dual protection against supply disruption and financial stress.

aud_usd_spot_rate_drops_952e2a.label: AUD/USD spot rate drops

MYR depreciated 2.7 sigma vs USD, smaller magnitude but directionally aligned.
EM currency ETF moved 3 sigma but post-mean is slightly above pre-mean, suggesting the peak deviation captured intra-window volatility rather than sustained selloff, partial contradiction.

energy_sector_equities_drop_76eb3c.description: E&P and integrated oil equities reprice lower as the WTI spot decline cuts expected revenue and free cash flow estimates for the quarter.

edges.mechanism: Extraterritorial REE controls injected supply chain tail risk, triggering a sharp VIX repricing alongside HY spread widening as markets discounted disruption to defense, EV, and semiconductor production.

supporting_data.note: VIX peaked roughly 9 points above pre event mean within 7 days, a textbook vol shock consistent with a supply scare.

Semis ultimately rallied into the window close, suggesting the vol spike was a transient fear pulse that faded as policy details emerged.
Rare earth miners rallied 5 days post event, confirming the REE supply channel is the proximate driver.

edges.mechanism: REE export control shock raised default risk premia for supply-chain exposed leveraged borrowers, widening HY OAS and pressuring HY ETF prices alongside a VIX spike.

supporting_data.note: HY OAS widened roughly 41 bps within one day of the announcement, an 8 sigma move versus the prior month.

edges.mechanism: REE control shock triggers risk-off flows into Treasuries, with VIX spiking and 10y yield compressing as duration is bid.

critique.counterargument: The supporting data conflates equity moves in non-Chinese producers (MP, LYC) with actual spot price prints for dysprosium/terbium/yttrium, but those are two different things. Equity rallies in MP and Lynas reflect expected future market share and pricing power, not realized spot price surges — and in fact REMX (closer to the actual rare earth complex) declined. Historically (e.g., the 2010-2011 China rare earth quotas), spot prices for heavy rare earths did spike, but the 2015 WTO-driven repeal saw spot prices crash even as Chinese export rhetoric stayed elevated, showing equity proxies frequently overshoot actual spot moves. Without direct spot price evidence (Asian Metal, Argus, Shanghai Metal Market quotes), 0.70 confidence is overclaim from equity proxies alone.

rebuttal.rebuttal: The adversary is correct that equity proxies are not spot prints, and that REMX's decline reflects Chinese-miner weighting rather than spot moves — this is a fair methodological caution. However, the 2010-2011 analog the adversary cites actually supports the link: heavy rare earth spot prices (dysprosium, terbium) rose 5-10x within months of China's quota tightening, and the April 2025 controls are narrower-targeted at exactly those heavy elements with license uncertainty, which is a tighter supply shock than broad quotas. The 2015 WTO repeal counter-example cuts the other way — it shows spot prices respond to actual supply availability, and licenses being withheld is a real availability constraint, not rhetoric. That said, the adversary is right that 0.70 confidence based on equity proxies alone is somewhat aggressive without direct Asian Metal/Argus prints, so the link survives but at modestly reduced confidence.

critique.counterargument: The premise that falling 2y yields drive MMF inflows is empirically backwards in recent cycles. During 2024, the 2y UST yield fell from ~5.0% to ~4.2% yet MMF assets rose to record highs of ~$6.5T (ICI data) — flows are driven by the absolute level of front-end yields versus deposit rates and equity risk appetite, not by the direction of the 2y. Conversely, in 2020-2021 when 2y yields collapsed to ~0.1%, MMF assets actually drained as yields became uncompetitive. The 'lock in yields before cuts' logic also fails because MMFs are floating-rate vehicles — they cannot lock in anything; their yields reset as the curve falls.

Rare earths are a narrow industrial input concentrated in defense/EV/semis, not a broad consumer-price shock like oil/gas/wheat in 2022 — they do not move headline CPI or breakevens materially (T10YIE barely reacted in prior rare-earth episodes like the 2010 China export quota and 2023 gallium/germanium controls, while 10y yields drifted lower on growth-scare framing). The cited 14bp drop with TLT rallying 5.3 sigma and DGS2 falling 16bp is the signature of duration/safety demand, not an inflation trade — in an inflation-dominated regime, the front end would sell off and TLT would underperform, the opposite of what occurred. critique.counterargument: The mechanism overstates the reflexive NAV-to-outflow loop in BKLN. Senior loan ETFs price off bid-side marks that lag actual secondary loan prices by days to weeks due to T+7+ loan settlement, so retail redemption signals are typically muted and delayed rather than 'accelerating.' More importantly, BKLN historically experiences inflows during early HY stress as investors rotate to floating-rate exposure to hedge duration; the 2022 Fed-hike drawdown saw HY OAS widen sharply while BKLN actually gathered assets. Without evidence of confirmed BKLN net outflows (the supporting data shows price decline, not redemption flow), the causal claim that NAV pressure 'forces' secondary selling is unsupported.

critique.counterargument: The transmission from leveraged loan secondary prices to auto-parts equity valuations requires the affected issuers to actually be refinancing or marking new debt at higher rates in the near term. Most leveraged loan borrowers have term loans with multi-year maturities, so secondary price drops do not immediately raise their cash interest expense — only the floating-rate base does, and that's already captured by SOFR independently. Equity investors typically react to leveraged loan secondary repricing only when a refinancing wall is imminent (e.g., 2024-2025 maturity wall episodes), not as a contemporaneous signal. The cited evidence (HY OAS, HYG) is generic credit stress, not auto-parts-specific loan repricing, so the named mechanism is largely redundant with broader credit/equity correlation already captured elsewhere in the graph.

critique.counterargument: The 'CNY weakness raises local-currency import cost' channel is largely inoperative for Chinese copper buyers because major state-linked importers and smelters routinely hedge USD exposure via SHFE/LME arbitrage and forward FX, and copper is priced and arbitraged in USD globally — so a 1-2% CNY move does not mechanically suppress global copper futures, which are set at the marginal USD price. The supporting_data conflates correlation with causation: DTWEXBGS, CPER, and FXI all moved together because broad USD strength and risk-off pressure both assets directly, not because CNY weakness transmitted to copper via import costs. The named mechanism is therefore largely a restatement of 'strong USD = weak commodities,' which is already captured upstream; the CNY-specific transmission claim is structurally weak.

Targeting equipment (not the oxides themselves) is an even more indirect channel — existing separation plants in Malaysia (Lynas), Estonia (Neo), and the US (MP Materials) already operate without new Chinese equipment, so near-term oxide supply is not mechanically choked. A specific +25% magnitude with no cited supporting_data is overclaim.

Picker: The safety-bid/risk-off compression mechanism requires that Treasuries still function as the dominant safe-haven duration asset, which is gated by the current yield level (ten_year) and the Fed's policy stance (fed_funds)—in a high-rate, quantitative-tightening regime the flight-to-quality bid is structurally weaker and competes with cash/money-market alternatives, whereas in a low-rate regime duration is readily bid on risk-off.

cs_4593cd18::usd_broad_index_strengthens.reasoning: avg normalized distance on ['dxy', 'ten_year'] = 0.22 (within threshold 1.5). Picker: The linkage fires through a risk-off capital flight channel: DXY directly measures whether USD is already in a strong-dollar regime (amplifying or muting further appreciation), while the 10-year yield signals whether safe-haven Treasury demand is reinforcing the dollar bid via falling yields and widening rate differentials versus EUR.

cs_bcdae6e4::2y_ust_yield_drops_sharply_.reasoning: avg normalized distance on ['fed_funds', 'ten_year'] = 0.96 (within threshold 1.5). Picker: The mechanism fires only when the Fed is in a restrictive hold (high fed_funds leaves room for cut re-pricing) and the long end (ten_year) is sensitive to growth-shock signals, so both the level of policy rates and the term structure determine whether a trade-war escalation can sharply compress the 2y yield by pulling forward easing expectations.

cs_bcdae6e4::10y_ust_yield_falls_on_grow.reasoning: avg normalized distance on ['ten_year', 'real_gdp_yoy'] = 1.29 (within threshold 1.5). Picker: The flight-to-quality/growth-fear channel requires long-end yields to have room to fall (i.e., not already pinned near zero by policy) and requires real growth to be fragile enough that a rare-earth supply shock credibly marks down the growth outlook, making current 10y yield levels and GDP momentum the key regime determinants.

cs_bcdae6e4::broad_usd_index_strengthens.reasoning: avg normalized distance on ['dxy', 'ten_year'] = 0.73 (within threshold 1.5). Picker: Safe-haven USD flows from trade-war uncertainty transmit directly through the broad dollar index (DXY), while the 10-year yield captures whether risk-off sentiment is simultaneously driving flight-to-quality bond demand that reinforces dollar strength.

cs_bcdae6e4::vix_spikes_on_supply_chain_.reasoning: avg normalized distance on ['ten_year', 'real_gdp_yoy'] = 1.29 (within threshold 1.5). Picker: When long-end yields are elevated and growth is slowing, equity risk premia are already compressed and tail-risk sentiment is more fragile, making supply chain shock signals more likely to spike VIX; a weakening growth backdrop amplifies fears that rare earth disruptions translate into tangible earnings/production hits for tech and defense.
